skill.name: multi-platform-apps-multi-platform
skill.description: Plan consistent feature delivery across web, mobile, and desktop using API-first contracts, shared architecture, and platform-specific implementation guidance.
skill.summary: Coordinate API-first multi-platform feature development across web, mobile, desktop, documentation, testing, and optimization.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, browser storage, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found only workflow prose in SKILL.md, with no executable shell commands, cryptographic code, browser storage access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt. The skill is safe to publish with low risk because it relies on user-provided project context and optional subagents.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Pattern
low_findings.description: Line 27 tells the assistant to open a local markdown playbook if detailed examples are needed. This is documentation navigation, not Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains a plain instruction to open a local resource path. No shell syntax, command interpolation, or executable code is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Browser Storage Access Pattern
low_findings.description: Line 108 recommends Room database for Android local persistence. It does not access browser storage, read secrets, or describe collecting sensitive data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is an Android architecture checklist item. There is no JavaScript storage API, token handling, or exfiltration path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Patterns
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain feature planning, platform names, design guidance, testing guidance, or configuration labels. No weak hash algorithm, encryption API, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of every flagged line found natural-language workflow instructions only. There is no cryptographic function call, algorithm name used as code, or security-sensitive implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Patterns
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss platform design details such as responsive layouts, Android implementation, testing outputs, app store requirements, and device fragmentation. They do not collect host data or run discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are headings or checklist text for application development. There are no commands such as uname, whoami, network scanning, or filesystem enumeration.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose feature parity when web, mobile, and desktop work happens separately. This skill guides API-first planning, shared architecture, parallel implementation, and validation across platforms.
actual_capabilities: Guides API contract planning with REST, GraphQL, WebSocket, authentication, validation, and error handling considerations.
Coordinates design system, shared business logic, and platform-specific implementation phases.
Provides platform prompts for web, iOS, Android, desktop, documentation, testing, and optimization work.
Defines configuration options for target platforms, API-first flow, shared code strategy, design systems, and testing scope.
Lists success criteria for feature parity, performance, accessibility, documentation, and code reuse.
limitations: This is a workflow guide, not an executable build tool or deployment system.
Referenced subagent names may not exist in every Claude, Codex, or Claude Code environment.
The optional resources/implementation-playbook.md file is referenced but is not included in this package.
It requires a clear feature specification and existing project context from the user.

use_cases.title: Plan a Shared Product Feature
use_cases.description: Define API contracts, shared models, design requirements, and implementation sequencing before teams begin platform work.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Parallel Platform Builds
use_cases.description: Give web, iOS, Android, and desktop contributors aligned prompts while preserving platform-specific conventions.

use_cases.title: Validate Release Readiness
use_cases.description: Create parity, performance, accessibility, and documentation checks before shipping a feature across platforms.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Multi-Platform Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a multi-platform implementation for this feature: [describe feature]. Target web, iOS, and Android. Identify the API contract, shared data model, UI requirements, and testing checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Create Platform Workstreams
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to split [feature] into platform workstreams. Include backend API tasks, web tasks, iOS tasks, Android tasks, documentation tasks, and integration checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Review Feature Parity
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review feature parity for [feature] across web, iOS, Android, and desktop. Produce a concise matrix covering behavior, UI, offline support, performance, accessibility, and known gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an Existing Rollout
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced improvement of an in-progress cross-platform release.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to improve an existing multi-platform rollout for [feature]. Analyze API reuse, shared logic, platform-specific optimizations, release risks, testing depth, and documentation gaps.

output_examples.input: Plan a saved search feature for web, iOS, and Android.
output: A phased plan starts with shared API endpoints for saving, listing, updating, and deleting searches.
The design track defines consistent filter controls, empty states, and accessibility rules for each platform.
The testing track validates sync behavior, offline handling, permissions, and parity across all target apps.

output_examples.input: Check whether a notifications feature is ready for multi-platform release.
output: The review compares delivery rules, settings screens, permission flows, and failure states across platforms.
It flags missing desktop notification behavior and incomplete Android accessibility coverage.
The release checklist requires parity fixes, updated API documentation, and final end-to-end tests.

best_practices: Define API contracts and shared data models before assigning platform implementation work.
Use platform conventions for UI while keeping behavior, terminology, and data rules consistent.
Validate parity with functional, performance, accessibility, and documentation checks before release.
anti_patterns: Starting platform builds before the API contract and error model are agreed.
Forcing identical UI patterns across platforms when native conventions differ.
Treating parity as a visual check instead of testing behavior, data sync, and edge cases.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete applications?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured workflow and prompts for planning, implementation coordination, testing, and optimization.

faq.question: Which platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers web, iOS, Android, and optional desktop builds using Electron or Tauri.

faq.question: Can I use it for only two platforms?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the platforms option in your prompt and omit workstreams that do not apply.

faq.question: Does it require a specific framework?
faq.answer: No. It suggests common frameworks, but you can adapt the workflow to your existing stack.

faq.question: How does it help with feature parity?
faq.answer: It defines shared contracts, implementation checkpoints, and validation criteria for consistent behavior across platforms.

faq.question: Is it safe to use in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found workflow text only, with no executable scripts or confirmed dangerous behavior.
